template_variables.product_vision: TinyURL is a frictionless, public URL shortening service ensuring links are instantly accessible and reliably tracked without requiring user accounts or complex setup. It empowers Link Sharers, Clickers, and API Clients through a secure, minimal architecture focused on speed and simplicity.
template_variables.intent_statement_summary:
Product: TinyURL is a frictionless, public URL shortening service ensuring links are instantly accessible and reliably tracked without requiring user accounts or complex setup. It empowers Link Sharers, Clickers, and API Clients through a secure, minimal architecture focused on speed and simplicity. — TinyURL is a minimal HTTP service that converts long URLs into short, six-character slugs for public sharing and instantly redirects traffic back to the original destination. It operates without user authentication, focusing purely on the core value of link compression and reliable delivery. The system leverages domains like the URL Shortening Engine, Threat Detection Gateway, and Encrypted Storage Layer to ensure security and performance, while integrations manage rate limiting, disaster recovery, and logging.
Who it serves: Link Sharer — Any user needing to share a long URL but wants to minimize space and clutter. Does not require login or persistence.
Problem: TinyURL is a minimal HTTP service that converts long URLs into short, six-character slugs for public sharing and instantly redirects traffic back to the original destination. It operates without user authentication, focusing purely on the core value of link compression and reliable delivery. The system leverages domains like the URL Shortening Engine, Threat Detection Gateway, and Encrypted Storage Layer to ensure security and performance, while integrations manage rate limiting, disaster recovery, and logging.
Confirmed assumptions: [{"assumption_id":"DEC-1","assumption":"No user accounts or authentication: Access granted via URL slug only, reducing friction for Link Sharers.","confirmed_by_record_id":"DEC-1"},{"assumption_id":"DEC-2","assumption":"Single-tenant architecture: The service does not support multi-tenancy, teams, or organizational hierarchies.","confirmed_by_record_id":"DEC-2"},{"assumption_id":"DEC-3","assumption":"Fixed-length slugs: All short URLs will be exactly 6 characters to ensure uniformity and compactness.","confirmed_by_record_id":"DEC-3"},{"assumption_id":"DEC-4","assumption":"No vanity slugs: Custom short URLs are out of scope for this slice to maintain automation and consistency.","confirmed_by_record_id":"DEC-4"}]
Confirmed constraints: ["HTTPS only on all public endpoints; HTTP requests must redirect to HTTPS.","No rate limiting on submission endpoints.","No bulk submission APIs allowed for this slice.","URLs stored at rest must be encrypted to protect incidentally collected PII.","Deletion requests from Sharers must be honored based on known slug."]
Out of scope: ["Should the service allow Sharers to pre-register vanity slugs or require manual approval for them?"]
template_variables.accepted_journeys:
- UJ-SUBMIT-NEW-LINK [Phase 1] (persona P-SHARER) User Submits New Short URL Request: P-SHARER pastes a long URL into the input field and submits a request to create a short link.
  Acceptance: Link created successfully within VV-REDIRECT-LATENCY threshold | No plaintext URLs found in storage (VV-SECURITY-ENCRYPTION) | Valid slug returned
- UJ-CLICK-SHORT-URL [Phase 1] (persona P-CLICKER) Clicker Navigates to Short Link: P-CLICKER receives a short URL and clicks it to reach the target destination.
  Acceptance: Redirect completes successfully | P95 latency ≤ 100ms | Target domain loaded
- UJ-REQUEST-QUOTA [Phase 1] (persona P-API-CLIENT) Client Requests Batch Link Creation: P-API-CLIENT script sends multiple POST requests to the API endpoint.
  Acceptance: API responds with HTTP 200 or 429 appropriately | All created links have unique slugs | No rate limit abuse
- UJ-ENFORCE-RATE-LIMIT [Phase 1] (persona P-API-CLIENT) Client Encounters Rate Limit: P-API-CLIENT hits DoS protection threshold and receives a 429 response.
  Acceptance: HTTP 429 returned correctly | Retry-After header accurate | Rate limit reset logic functions
- UJ-REQUEST-GDPR-EXPORT [Phase 1] (persona P-SHARER) Sharer Requests Data Export: EU-based P-SHARER requests their data under GDPR Right to Access.
  Acceptance: Export file contains all stored link data | GDPR compliance maintained | Process completes within retention window
- UJ-REQUEST-PII-DELETION [Phase 1] (persona P-SHARER) Sharer Requests Right to be Forgotten: P-SHARER invokes deletion right for their specific slug.
  Acceptance: Data purged from storage and cache | Audit logs sanitized | Compliance with COMP-PII-SANCTION
- UJ-MARKET-TRACK-CLICKS [Phase 1] (persona P-MARKETING-MGR) Manager Views Campaign Analytics: P-MARKETING-MGR accesses analytics dashboard for a specific tracking link.
  Acceptance: Click counts accurate | Latency within VV-REDIRECT-LATENCY | Data not exposed to unauthorized users
- UJ-MARKET-CREATE-CAMPAIGN [Phase 1] (persona P-MARKETING-MGR) Manager Creates Tracking Link: P-MARKETING-MGR uses the public shortener for campaign tracking.
  Acceptance: Link functions as expected | Tracking enabled correctly
- UJ-OPS-MONITOR-HEALTH [Phase 1] (persona P-PLATFORM-OPS) Ops Team Monitors Service Uptime: P-PLATFORM-OPS reviews system metrics to ensure availability targets.
  Acceptance: No downtime detected | Latency within VV-REDIRECT-LATENCY
- UJ-OPS-TRIGGER-RECOVERY [Phase 1] (persona P-PLATFORM-OPS) Ops Team Initiates Disaster Recovery: P-PLATFORM-OPS detects regional failure and triggers RTO plan.
  Acceptance: Recovery Time Objective met (≤15 mins) | Data integrity verified
- UJ-HOST-ENFORCE-HTTPS [Phase 1] (persona P-SHARER) System Enforces HTTPS on HTTP Request: P-SHARER attempts to use HTTP endpoint, system redirects to HTTPS.
  Acceptance: HTTPS enforced | No HTTP data leaked
- UJ-SEC-REJECT-MALICIOUS [Phase 1] (persona P-SHARER) System Blocks Malicious URL Submission: P-SHARER submits a link known or detected to be malicious by security filter.
  Acceptance: Malicious link blocked before storage | User receives clear rejection message
- UJ-RETENTION-NOTIFY-EXPIRY [Phase 1] (persona P-MARKETING-MGR) System Notifies Manager of Retention Policy Expiry: P-MARKETING-MGR receives notice that their tracking link is subject to deletion per policy.
  Acceptance: User notified before data loss | User can extend or accept deletion
- UJ-OPS-AUDIT-LOG-REVIEW [Phase 1] (persona P-PLATFORM-OPS) Ops Team Reviews System Audit Logs: P-PLATFORM-OPS inspects logs for debugging or compliance verification.
  Acceptance: All logs conform to structured JSON | Immutable integrity maintained
- UJ-SECURITY-ENCRYPT-STORE [Phase 1] (persona P-PLATFORM-OPS) System Encrypts Stored URL at Rest: System step ensuring compliance with AES-256 storage requirement.
  Acceptance: Plaintext never exposed in DB | Encryption key rotated securely
- UJ-CLICK-FAILURE-TIMEOUT [Phase 1] (persona P-CLICKER) Clicker Encounters Redirect Timeout: P-CLICKER attempts to access a link that times out during redirect logic.
  Acceptance: User receives timeout notification | No data loss or crash
template_variables.accepted_entities:
- ENT-LINK (DOM-URL-SHORTENER) Link Definition: Core entity representing a submitted short link and its target destination.
- ENT-SLUG-CONFIG (DOM-SLUG-GENERATOR) Slug Generation Configuration: Defines how unique slugs are generated, including prefix rules and character sets.
- ENT-SLUG-POOL (DOM-SLUG-GENERATOR) Slug Pool Registry: Track active slug tokens to prevent collisions and manage expiration.
- ENT-REDIRECT-CONFIG (DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE) Redirect Configuration: Settings controlling HTTP 302 responses and destination validation rules.
- ENT-REDIRECT-LOG (DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE) Redirect Execution Log: Records every successful redirect execution including latency and status.
- ENT-CLICK-EVENT (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS) Click Event Record: Individual click tracking for real-time analytics and attribution.
- ENT-CAMPAIGN (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS) Marketing Campaign: A group of links tracked together for marketing manager reporting.
- ENT-CAMPAIGN-STAT (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS) Campaign Aggregate Statistics: Pre-aggregated metrics for the marketing manager dashboard to improve performance.
- ENT-IP-GEO-DATA (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS) IP Geolocation Data: Map IP addresses to geographic regions for analytics visualization.
- ENT-ENCRYPTED-PAYLOAD (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT) Encrypted Target Payload: The encrypted version of the original long URL stored at rest.
- ENT-DECRYPTION-META (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT) Decryption Metadata: Metadata required to safely decrypt the payload during redirect.
- ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR (DOM-SECURITY-FILTER) Threat Indicator Registry: Database of known malicious patterns and IPs to block redirects.
- ENT-SCAN-RESULT (DOM-SECURITY-FILTER) Security Scan Result: Result of a security scan for a specific incoming URL.
- ENT-BLOCKLIST-RULE (DOM-SECURITY-FILTER) Blocklist Rule: Configuration rules for IP ranges or domains to block.
- ENT-API-KEY (DOM-API-GATEWAY) API Access Key: Credentials for external clients to access the public API.
- ENT-API-CLIENT (DOM-API-GATEWAY) API Client Profile: Profile of an automated system interacting with the service.
- ENT-AUTH-TOKEN (DOM-API-GATEWAY) Authentication Token: Transient token for session management and client validation.
- ENT-RATE-LIMIT-BUCKET (DOM-RATE-LIMITER) Rate Limit Bucket: State container for rate limiting per client or IP address.
- ENT-RATE-LIMIT-CONFIG (DOM-RATE-LIMITER) Rate Limit Configuration: Thresholds and window sizes for preventing DoS and abuse.
- ENT-IP-SCORE (DOM-RATE-LIMITER) IP Reputation Score: Dynamic score of an IP address based on request patterns.
- ENT-ABUSE-REPORT (DOM-RATE-LIMITER) Abuse Incident Report: Log of abusive behaviors detected by the rate limiting system.
- ENT-CACHE-ENTRY (DOM-CACHE-LAYER) Cache Key Entry: Stored short URL mapping in the performance cache layer.
- ENT-CACHE-TASK (DOM-CACHE-LAYER) Cache Management Task: Scheduled job to populate cache or warm specific keys.
- ENT-CACHE-EVICT-LOG (DOM-CACHE-LAYER) Cache Eviction Log: Track when and why cache entries were removed for capacity.
- ENT-AUDIT-RECORD (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING) Audit Log Record: Immutable record of API calls, errors, and system events.
- ENT-AUDIT-INDEX (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING) Audit Index Map: Efficient index structure for querying audit logs by date or type.
- ENT-GDPR-REQUEST (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING) GDPR Compliance Request: Record of Data Subject Access Requests for export or deletion.
- ENT-BACKUP-JOB (DOM-BACKUP-OPS) Backup Execution Job: Task representing a scheduled or manual backup run.
- ENT-SNAPSHOT-FILE (DOM-BACKUP-OPS) Backup Snapshot: Physical or logical container holding the backup data.
- ENT-RECOVERY-PLAN (DOM-BACKUP-OPS) Disaster Recovery Plan: Procedure and resource map for restoring service after failure.
- ENT-POLICY-RULE (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY) Data Retention Rule: Configuration for how long link data must be kept.
- ENT-RETENTION-TASK (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY) Retention Cleanup Task: Automated job to delete expired data per policy rules.
- ENT-LIFECYCLE-LOG (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY) Link Lifecycle Event Log: History of a link's life including creation and deletion events.
- ENT-COMPLIANCE-DATA (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING) Compliance Data Set: Structured data specifically formatted for regulatory exports.
- ENT-SYSTEM-CLOCK (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING) System Clock Reference: NTP synchronized time reference for log ordering.
- ENT-NOTIFICATION-TPL (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY) Notification Template: Text templates used for expiry notifications to managers.
- ENT-COMMIT-LOG (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT) Storage Commit Log: Audit of encryption key rotation and storage integrity checks.
- ENT-REGION-MAPPING (DOM-CACHE-LAYER) Region Endpoint Map: Configuration for where cache nodes reside geographically.
- ENT-HEALTH-METRIC (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING) System Health Metric: Current operational status of the infrastructure components.
- ENT-DISASTER-STATE (DOM-BACKUP-OPS) Disaster Recovery State: Current state of active disaster recovery or failover.
- ENT-FEATURE-FLAG (DOM-RATE-LIMITER) Feature Flag Record: Toggle switches for enabling new features like advanced analytics.
- ENT-CONFIG-VERSION (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY) Configuration Version: Snapshot of system configuration at a specific point in time.
- ENT-METADATA-DEF (DOM-URL-SHORTENER) Link Metadata Definition: Schema definition for the data fields attached to a link.
- ENT-REDIRECT-TIMEOUT (DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE) Redirect Timeout Record: Logs of redirects that failed due to network latency or timeout.
- ENT-PAYLOAD-SIZE (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT) Encrypted Payload Size Tracker: Monitors the size of stored data to enforce storage limits.
template_variables.compliance_extracted_items:
- COMP-GDPR-JURISDICTION [CONSTRAINT] The service is offered globally; GDPR applies to EU sharers.
- COMP-AES-256-REST [CONSTRAINT] Stored URLs encrypted at rest using AES-256 because the URLs may incidentally contain personally identifying or session tokens.
- COMP-PII-SANCTION [CONSTRAINT] Treat all stored URLs as sensitive: encrypt at rest (NFR-2), and accept a deletion request from a sharer who knows the slug.
- COMP-HTTPS-ENFORCEMENT [CONSTRAINT] Transport: HTTPS only on all public endpoints; HTTP requests redirect to HTTPS.
template_variables.canonical_vocabulary:
- VOC-TINYURL TinyURL: TinyURL is a public-facing service that converts a long URL into a short, shareable URL and redirects requests for the short URL back to the original destination.
- VOC-SHORT-URL Short URL: A short, shareable URL generated by the service.
- VOC-ORIGINAL-URL Original URL: The destination URL stored in the mapping.
- VOC-CLICK-COUNTER Click Counter: A metric that increments per redirect issued.
- VOC-VANITY-SLUGS Vanity Slugs: Custom slugs requested by the user.
- VOC-HEALTH-CHECK-ENDPOINT Health-check Endpoint: An endpoint that exists and is monitored externally.
- VOC-DELETION-REQUEST Deletion Request: A request from a sharer to delete their mapped URL.
- VOC-SLUG slug: A unique 6-character string generated to identify a URL mapping.
template_variables.open_questions:
- Q-1 [OPEN_QUESTION] What is the data retention policy for URLs after a Sharer deletes their slug? Is there a grace period before permanent deletion?
- Q-2 [OPEN_QUESTION] How should the system detect and handle malicious URLs or known phishing targets?
- Q-3 [OPEN_QUESTION] Should the service allow Sharers to pre-register vanity slugs or require manual approval?
- Q-4 [OPEN_QUESTION] Are there specific industries or verticals (e.g., healthcare, finance) that require additional handling or certification?
